decisions.decision: Exclude WebSocket endpoints entirely from the spec; mention them only in the info.description section
alternatives_rejected: Document WebSocket endpoints as stubs with GET+Upgrade operations
Create a separate AsyncAPI spec for WebSocket endpoints

decisions.decision: Preserve the actual wire format with mixed casing (snake_case for temporal fields, camelCase for identifiers) exactly as the backend emits
Document only primary JSON responses; omit HTML content-negotiation variants and treat idempotency replay as an optional 'replayed' field in meta
alternatives_rejected: Document all content-negotiation variants with multiple media types
Create separate response schemas for replay vs fresh

decisions.decision: Document rate-limiting as conditional behavior in the description; include 429 as a possible response on write endpoints but do not make X-RateLimit-* headers required on all responses
alternatives_rejected: Add X-RateLimit-* headers to every endpoint response

decisions.decision: Include only REST request/response schemas in components; exclude WebSocket message types and client-only types
alternatives_rejected: Include all types from types.ts including WebSocket message schemas
Create a separate schemas file for non-REST types

decisions.decision: Use OpenAPI 3.0.3 in a single docs/openapi.yaml file with internal $ref paths only
alternatives_rejected: Upgrade to OpenAPI 3.1 for native JSON Schema support
Split into multiple files with external $ref

steps.task: Audit existing docs/openapi.yaml paths against backend router (server.py:4910-5115) to identify any missing endpoints, incorrect methods, or stale path definitions
Compare existing schema components against frontend/src/lib/api/types.ts and backend response handler functions to identify missing or outdated fields in request/response schemas
Update path definitions: fix any missing query parameters (e.g., pagination params on list endpoints, filter params on analytics), correct response status codes, and add any undocumented endpoints
Update component schemas: add missing properties to existing schemas, fix type mismatches (nullable fields, enum values), and add any missing schema definitions referenced by updated paths
Validate the final spec by running it through a linter (if available) or manual structural verification — ensure all $ref targets resolve, required fields are consistent, and examples are valid

decomposition_assessment.decision_reason: Estimated total lines (220) is well under 500. Zero high-risk steps. While steps 1-2 and 3-4 are logically parallel, they all modify the same output file (docs/openapi.yaml), making true parallel execution impractical. The existing spec is already substantially complete at 1814 lines — this is an audit-and-update task, not a from-scratch generation. A single agent can handle this efficiently.
